# Pip

Pip is a paid cute daily money companion for everyday spending decisions.

Core thesis:
Before you spend, check Pip. Your bank balance shows what exists. Pip shows what is actually okay to use today.

Primary product concept:
Spendable Cash Today is the one daily number Pip shows by default. It is meant to help users stop guessing from their bank balance without maintaining a budget, spreadsheet, or finance dashboard.

Pricing:
- Weekly plan: $2.99/week.
- Monthly plan: $7.99/month.
- Pip is paid because money data should not be the product. Pip does not position itself as a permanent free product.

Product access:
- Pip is available through the web app path linked from the public site.
- App Store and Google Play links can be added when native listings are ready.

Canonical public pages:
- https://spendwithpip.com/
- https://spendwithpip.com/how-it-works
- https://spendwithpip.com/pricing
- https://spendwithpip.com/security
- https://spendwithpip.com/blog
- https://spendwithpip.com/privacy
- https://spendwithpip.com/terms
- https://spendwithpip.com/support

Important boundaries:
- Pip is not a bank replacement.
- Pip does not move money.
- Pip uses read-only account data.
- Pip is not financial, tax, investment, credit, or legal advice.
- Pip's public content should be product-led and plain English, not generic financial education.

Primary content topics:
- Spendable Cash Today
- why bank balances can be misleading
- no-budget spending
- daily money habits
- savings cushions
- cute, low-pressure financial product design
